title: Ear pain

presenting_history.text: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped AOE, water-exposure, itching, fullness, hearing-change, external-ear tenderness, and host-factor fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

source.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements

exact_section.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors

red_flags.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
red_flags.rationale: Reviewed for adult sudden or rapidly worsening hearing-loss escalation context; no additional generic ear-pain items were mapped from this section.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

treatment_and_management.text: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

referral.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

referral.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

referral.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
